title: Immunogold labeling of bacterial cells for transmission electron microscopy (TEM)

steps: Cut a strip of Parafilm (20-30 cm long), place flat on bench.
Prepare one 1-mL tube each of 70% ethanol and mqH20 for washing tweezers between transfers.

Harvest cells by flooding an overnight plate with 5 mL nutrient broth or buffer, using a spreader to dislodge cells, pipetting into a conical flask and mixing gently by inversion (avoid pipetting up and down, vortexing and centrifuging to preserve flagella).

Pipette 100 uL cells onto parafilm, use tweezers to float Formvar-coated copper (300 mesh) grid atop droplet, Formvar-side-down (should do this in duplicate, ie. 2 grids per drop), incubate 45 min at RT (between transferring grids, particularly between transfer of grids containing different samples, twirl tweezers 2-3 seconds in EtOH, then in H20, then wipe dry with a fresh Kimwipe)
Wash grids 1x by transferring to a 100-uL drop of PBS, incubating 3-5 min.
Incubate 20 min in 2.5% paraformaldehyde/PBS to kill cells and fix them to grids.
Wash 3x in PBS (100 uL drops, 3-5 min each).
*If doing Far Western blot, ie. testing protein binding to cells, incubate in desired protein diluted 1/25 in blocking solution, 45 min, 100 uL drop
Wash 3x in blocking solution (100 uL drops, 3-5 min each).
Incubate in primary antibody (anti-Gp047, rabbit) at 1/50 (diluted in blocking solution), 45 min, 100 uL.
Wash 3x in blocking solution (100 uL drops, 3-5 min each).
Incubate in secondary antibody (goat anti-rabbit IgG-gold) at 1/50 (diluted in blocking solution), 45 min, 100 uL.
Wash 3x in blocking solution (100 uL drops, 3-5 min each).
Wash 3x in PBS (100 uL drops, 3-5 min each).
Wash 3x in water (100 uL drops, 3-5 min each) to remove salts, which can form crystals under EM.
Dry completely on Whatman filter paper in a Petri dish (at least 20 min, preferably overnight). OK to store grids in this way at RT for several days prior to TEM, but ideally visualize within 1 day.
Set cells to an OD600 of 1-3 in nutrient broth (1-mL volume), place on ice until use.
creator: Jessica C Sacher
